metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 63

page_content: You an find the money in that drawer.’ Then he resumed his recitation.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 63

page_content: A little while afterwards he stopped and called: ‘Don’t take it all.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 63

page_content: I need some to pay taxes with tomorrow.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 63

page_content: The intruder gathered up most of the money and started to leave.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 63

page_content: ‘Thank a person when you receive a gift,’ Shichiri added.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 63

page_content: The man thanked him and made off.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 63

page_content: A few days afterwards the fellow was caught and confessed, among others, the offence against Shichiri.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 63

page_content: When Shichiri was called as a witness he said: ‘This man is no thief, at least as far as I am concerned.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 5, sentence number 3 on page 63

page_content: I gave him the money and he thanked me for it.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 44. The Thief Who Became a Disciple, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 63

page_content: After he had finished his prison term, the men went to Shichiri and became his disciple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 64

page_content: When Bankei held his seclusion weeks of meditation, pupils from many parts of Japan came to attend.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 64

page_content: During one of the gatherings a pupil was caught stealing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 64

page_content: The matter was reported to Bankei with the request that the culprit be expelled.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 64

page_content: Bankei ignored the case.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 64

page_content: Later the pupil was caught in a similar act, and again Bankei disregarded the matter.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 64

page_content: This angered the other pupils, who drew up a petition asking for the dismissal of the thief, stating that otherwise they would leave in a body.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 64

page_content: When Bankei had read the petition he called everyone before him.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 64

page_content: ‘You are wise brothers,’ he told them.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 64

page_content: ‘You know what is right and what is not right.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 64

page_content: You may go somewhere else to study if you wish, but this poor brother does not even know right from wrong.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 5 on page 64

page_content: Who will teach him if I do not?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 4, sentence number 6 on page 64

page_content: I am going to keep him here even if all the rest of you leave.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 64

page_content: A torrent of tears cleansed the face of the brother who had stolen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 45. Right and Wrong, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 64

page_content: All the desire to steal had vanished.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 65

page_content: During the Kamkura period, Shinkan studied Tendai six years and then studied Zen seven years; then he went to China and contemplated Zen for thirteen years more.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 65

page_content: When he returned to Japan many desired to interview him and asked obscure questions.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 65

page_content: But when Shinkan received visitors, which was infrequently, he seldom answered their questions.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 65

page_content: One day a fifty-year-old student of enlightenment said to Shinkan: ‘I have studied the Tendai school of thought since I was a little boy, but one thing in it I cannot understand.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 65

page_content: Tendai claims that even the grass and trees will become enlightened.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 65

page_content: To me this seems very strange.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 65

page_content: ‘Of what use is it to discuss how grass and trees become enlightened?’ asked Shinkan.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 65

page_content: The question is how you yourself can become so.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 65

page_content: Did you ever consider that!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 65

page_content: ‘I never thought of it in that way,’ marveled the old man.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. How Grass and Trees Become Enlightened, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 65

page_content: Then go home and think it over,’ finished Shinkan.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: Gessen was an artist monk.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: Before he would start a drawing or painting he always insisted upon being paid in advance and his fees were high.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 66

page_content: He was known as the ‘Stingy Artist’.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: A geisha once gave him a commission for a painting.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: ‘How much can you pay?’ inquired Gessen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 66

page_content: ‘Whatever you charge,’ replied the girl, ‘but I want you to do the work in front of me.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: So on a certain day Gessen was called by the geisha.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: She was holding a feast for her patron.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: Gessen with fine brushwork did the painting.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: When it was completed he asked the highest sum of his time.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 66

page_content: He received his pay.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 66

page_content: Then the geisha turned to her patron, saying: ‘All this artist wants is money.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 5 on page 66

page_content: His paintings are fine but his mind is dirty: money has caused it to become muddy.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 6 on page 66

page_content: Drawn by such a filthy mind his work is not fit to exhibit.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 4, sentence number 7 on page 66

page_content: It is just about good enough for one of my petticoats.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: Removing her skirt, she then asked Gessen to do another picture on the back of her petticoat.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: ‘How much will you pay?’ asked Gessen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: ‘Oh, any amount,’ answered the girl.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: Gessen named a fancy price, painted the picture in the manner requested and went away.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: It was learned later that Gessen had these reasons for desiring money.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 10, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: A ravaging famine often visited his province.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 10, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: The rich would not help the poor, so Gessen had a secret warehouse, unknown to anyone, which he kept filled with grain prepared for these emergencies.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 11, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: From his village to the National Shrine the road was in very poor condition and many travelers suffered while traversing it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 11, sentence number 2 on page 66

page_content: He desired to build a better road.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 12, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: His teacher had passed away without realizing his wish build a temple and Gessen wished to complete this temple for him.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 46. The Stingy Artist, paragraph number 13, sentence number 1 on page 66

page_content: After Gessen had accomplished his three wishes he threw away his brushes and artist’s materials and retiring to the mountains never painted again.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 68

page_content: Sen no Rikyu, a tea-master, wished to hang a flower basket on a column.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 68

page_content: He asked a carpenter to help him, directing the man to place it a little higher or lower, to the right or left, until he had found exactly the right spot.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 68

page_content: That’s the place,’ said Sen no Rikyu finally.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 68

page_content: The carpenter, to test the master, marked the spot and then pretended he had forgotten.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 68

page_content: Was this the place?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 68

page_content: ‘Was this the place, perhaps?’ the carpenter kept asking, pointing to various places on the column.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 47. Accurate Proportion, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 68

page_content: But so accurate was the tea-master’s sense of proportion that it was not until the carpenter reached the identical spot again that its location was approved.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 69

page_content: A nun who was searching for enlightenment made a statue of Buddha and covered it with gold leaf.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 69

page_content: Wherever she went she carried this golden Buddha with her.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 69

page_content: Years passed and, still carrying her Buddha, the nun came to live in a small temple in a country where there were many Buddha’s, each one with its own particular shrine.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 69

page_content: The nun wished to burn incense before her golden Buddha.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 69

page_content: Not liking the idea of the perfume straying to the others, she devised a funnel through which the smoke would ascend only to her statue.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 48. Black-Nosed Buddha, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 69

page_content: This blackened the nose of the Golden Buddha making it especially ugly.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: The Buddhist nun known as Ryonen was born in 1797.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: She was a granddaughter of the famous Japanese warrior Shingen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Her poetical genius and alluring beauty were such that at seventeen she was serving the empress as one of the ladies of the court.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: Even at such a youthful age fame awaited her.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: The beloved emperor died suddenly and Ryonen’s hopeful dreams vanished.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: She became acutely aware of the impermanency of life in this world.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 70

page_content: I was then that she desired to study Zen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Her relatives disagreed, however, and practically forced her into marriage.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: With a promise that she might become a nun after she had borne three children.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 70

page_content: Ryonen assented.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 70

page_content: Before she was twenty-five she had accomplished this condition.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Then her husband and relatives could no longer dissuade her from her desire.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: She shaved her had took the name of Ryonen which means to realize clearly, and stated on her pilgrimage.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 5, sentence number 3 on page 70

page_content: She came to the city of Edo and asked Tetsugyu to accept her as a disciple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 5, sentence number 4 on page 70

page_content: At one glance the master rejected her because she was too beautiful.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Ryonen then went to another master, Hakuo.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: Hakuo refused her for the same reason, saying that her beauty would only make trouble.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 6, sentence number 3 on page 70

page_content: Ryonen obtained a hot iron and placed it against her face.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 6, sentence number 4 on page 70

page_content: In a few moments her beauty had vanished forever.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Hakuo then accepted her as a disciple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Commemorating this occasion.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 8, sentence number 2 on page 70

page_content: Ryonen wrote a poem on the back of a little mirror:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: In the service of my Empress I burned incense to perfume my exquisite clothes,

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 10, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Now as a homeless mendicant I burn my face to enter a Zen temple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 11, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: When Ryonen was about to pass from this world she wrote another poem:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 12, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Sixty-six times have these eyes beheld the changing scene of autumn.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 13, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: I have had enough about moonlight,

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 50. Ryonen’s Clear Realization, paragraph number 14, sentence number 1 on page 70

page_content: Ask no more. Only listen to the voice of pines and cedars when no wind stirs.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 72

page_content: The cook monk Dairyo, at Bankei’s monastery, decided that he would take good care of his old teacher’s health and give him only fresh miso, a paste of soy beans mixed with wheat and yeast that often ferments.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 72

page_content: Bankei, noticing that he was being served better miso than his pupils, asked: ‘Who is the cook today?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 72

page_content: Dairyo was sent before him.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 72

page_content: Bankei learned that according to his age and position he should eat only fresh miso.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 72

page_content: So he said to the cook: Then you think I shouldn’t eat at all.’ With this he entered his room and locked the door.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 72

page_content: Dairyo, sitting outside the door, asked his teacher’s pardon.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 72

page_content: Bankei would not answer.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 72

page_content: For seven days Dairyo sit outside and Bankei within.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 72

page_content: Finally in desperation an adherent called loudly to Bankei: ‘You may be all right old teacher, but this young disciple here has to eat.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 72

page_content: He cannot go without food forever!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 72

page_content: At that Bankei opened the door.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 72

page_content: He was smiling.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 5, sentence number 3 on page 72

page_content: He told Dairyo: ‘I insist on eating the same food as the least of my followers.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 51. Sour Miso, paragraph number 5, sentence number 4 on page 72

page_content: When you become the teacher I do not want you to forget this.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 52. Your Light may go out, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 73

page_content: A student of Tendai a philosophical school of Buddhism, came to the Zen abode of Gasan as a pupil.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 52. Your Light may go out, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 73

page_content: When he was departing a few years later, Gasan warned him: ‘Studying the truth speculatively is useful as a way of collecting preaching material.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 52. Your Light may go out, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 73

page_content: But remember that unless you meditate constantly your light of truth may go out.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: While Seisetsu was the master of Engaku in Kamakura he required larger quarters, since those in which he was teaching were overcrowded.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 74

page_content: Umezu Seibei, a merchant of Edo, decided to donate five hundred pieces of gold called ryo toward the construction of a more commodious school.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 74

page_content: This money he brought to the teacher.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: Seisetsu said: ‘All right.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 74

page_content: I will take it.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: Umezu gave Seisetsu the sack of gold, but he was dissatisfied with the attitude of the teacher.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 74

page_content: One might live a whole year on three ryo, and the merchant had not even been thanked for five hundred.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: ‘In that sack are five hundred ryo,’ hinted Umezu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: “You told me that before,’ replied Seisetsu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: ‘Even if I am a wealthy merchant, five hundred ryo is a lot of money,’ said Umezu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: ‘Do you want me to thank you for it?’ asked Seisetsu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: “You ought to,’ replied Umezu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 74

page_content: ‘Why should I?’ inquired Seisetsu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 53. The Giver Should be Thankful, paragraph number 9, sentence number 2 on page 74

page_content: ‘The giver should be thankful.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: Ikkyu, a famous Zen teacher of the Ashikaga era, was the son of the emperor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 75

page_content: When he was very young, his mother left the palace and went to study Zen in a temple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 75

page_content: In this way Prince Ikkyu also became a student.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 75

page_content: When his mother passed on, she left with him a letter.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 1, sentence number 5 on page 75

page_content: It read:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: To Ikkyu:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: I have finished my work in this life and am now returning Into Eternity.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 75

page_content: I wish you to become a good student and to realize your Buddha-nature.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 75

page_content: You will know if I am in hell and whether I am always with you or not.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: If you become a man who realizes that the Buddha and his follower Bodhidharma are your own servants, you may leave off studying and work for humanity.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 75

page_content: The Buddha preached for forty-nine years and in all that time found it not necessary to speak one word.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 75

page_content: You ought to know why.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 75

page_content: But if you don’t and yet wish to, avoid thinking fruitlessly.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: Your Mother,

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: Not born, not dead.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: September first.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 75

page_content: PS.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 8, sentence number 2 on page 75

page_content: The teaching of Buddha was mainly for the purpose of enlightening others.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 8, sentence number 3 on page 75

page_content: If you are dependent a any of its methods, you are naught but an ignorant insect.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 8, sentence number 4 on page 75

page_content: There are 80,000, books on Buddhism and if you should read all of them and still not see your own nature, you will not understand even this letter.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 54. Last Will and Testament, paragraph number 8, sentence number 5 on page 75

page_content: This is my will and testament.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: Taiko, a warrior who lived in Japan before the Tokugawa era, studied Cha- no-yu, tea etiquette, with Sen no Rikyu, a teacher of that aesthetical expression of calmness and contentment.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: Taiko’s attendant warrior Kato interpreted his superior’s enthusiasm for tea etiquette as negligence of state affairs, m he decided to kill Sen no Rikyu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 76

page_content: He pretended to make a social call upon the tea-master and was invited to drink tea.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: The master, who was well skilled in his art, saw at a glance the warrior’s intention, so he invited Kato to leave his sword outside before entering the room for the ceremony, explaining that Cha-no-yu represents peacefulness itself.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: Kato would wt Isten to this ‘I am a warrior,’ he said.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 76

page_content: I always have my sword with me.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 76

page_content: Cha-no-yu or no Cha-no-yu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 76

page_content: I have my sword.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: ‘Very well.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 76

page_content: Bring your sword in and have some tea,’ consented Sen no Rikyu.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: The kettle was boiling on the charcoal fire.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 76

page_content: Suddenly Sen no Rikyu tipped it over.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 6, sentence number 3 on page 76

page_content: Hissing steam arose filling the room with smoke and ashes.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 6, sentence number 4 on page 76

page_content: The startled warrior ran outside.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: The tea-master apologized.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 76

page_content: ‘It is my mistake.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 7, sentence number 3 on page 76

page_content: Come back in and have some tea.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 7, sentence number 4 on page 76

page_content: I have your sword here covered with ashes and will clean it and give it to you.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 55. The Tea-Master and the Assassin, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 76

page_content: In this predicament the warrior realized he could not very well kill the tea-master, so he gave up the idea.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 77

page_content: Just before Ninakawa passed away the Zen master Ikkyu visited him.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 77

page_content: ‘Shall I lead you on Ikkyu asked.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 77

page_content: Ninakawa replied: ‘I came here alone and I go alone.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 77

page_content: What help could you be to me?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 77

page_content: Ikkyu answered: ‘If you think you really come and go, that is your delusion.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 77

page_content: Let me show you the path on which there is no coming and no going.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 56. The True Path, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 77

page_content: With his words Ikkyu had revealed the path so clearly that Ninakawa smiled and passed away.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: A soldier named Nobushige came to Hakuin, and asked: ‘Is there really a paradise and a hell?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: ‘Who are you?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 78

page_content: ‘ Inquired Hakuin.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: ‘I am a samurai,’ the warrior replied.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: ‘You, a soldier!’ exclaimed Hakuin.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 78

page_content: ‘What kind of ruler would have you as his guard?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 78

page_content: Your face looks like that of a beggar.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: Nobushige became so angry that he began to draw his sword, but Hakuin continued: ‘So you have a sword!

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 78

page_content: Your weapon is probably much too dull to cut off my head.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: As Nohushige drew his sword Hakuin remarked: ‘Here open the gates of hell!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: At these words the samurai, perceiving the master’s discipline, sheathed his sword and bowed.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 57. The Gates of Paradise, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 78

page_content: ‘Here open the gates of paradise,’ said Hakuin.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: A merchant bearing fifty rolls of cotton goods on his shoulder stopped to rest from the heat of the day beneath a shelter when a large stone Buddha was standing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: Then he fell asleep, and when he awoke his goods had disappeared.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 79

page_content: He immediately reported the matter to the police.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: A judge named O-oka opened court to investigate.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: ‘That stone Buddha must have stolen the goods,’ concluded the judge.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: ‘He is supposed to care for the welfare of the people but he has failed to perform his holy duty.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: Arrest him’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: The police arrested the stone Buddha and carried it into the court.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: A noisy crowd followed the statue; curious to learn what kind of a sentence the judge was about to impose.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: When O-oka appeared on the bench he rebuked the boisterous audience.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: “What right have you people to appear before the court laughing and joking in this manner?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 5, sentence number 3 on page 79

page_content: You are in contempt of court and subject to a fine and imprisonment.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: The people hastened to apologize.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: “I shall have to impose a fine on you,’ said the judge,’ but I will remit it provided each one of you brings one roll, of cotton goods to the court within three days.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 6, sentence number 3 on page 79

page_content: Anyone failing to do this will be arrested.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 79

page_content: One of the rolls of cloth, which the people brought, was quickly recognized by the merchant as his own, and thus the thief was easily discovered.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 58. Arresting the Stone Buddha, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 79

page_content: The merchant recovered his goods, and the cotton rolls were returned to the people.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 80

page_content: Once a division of the Japanese army was engaged in I sham battle, and some of the officers found it necessary to make their headquarters in Gasan’s temple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 80

page_content: Gasan told his cook: ‘Let the officers have only the same simple fare we eat.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 80

page_content: This made the army men angry, as they were used to very differential treatment.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 80

page_content: One came to Gasan and said: ‘Who do you think we are?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 80

page_content: We are soldiers, sacrificing our lives for our country.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 3, sentence number 4 on page 80

page_content: Why don’t you treat us accordingly?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 80

page_content: Gasan answered sternly: ‘Who do you think we are?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 59. Soldiers of Humanity, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 80

page_content: We are soldiers of humanity aiming to save all sentient beings.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: Zenkai, the son of a samurai, journeyed to Edo and there became the retainer of a high official.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: He fell in love with the official’s wife and was discovered.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 81

page_content: In self-defense, he slew the official.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 81

page_content: Then he ran away with the wife.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: Both of them later became thieves.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: But the woman was so greedy that Zenkai grew disgusted.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 81

page_content: Finally, leaving her, he journeyed far away to the province of Buzen, where he became a wandering mendicant.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: To atone for his past, Zenkai resolved to accomplish some good deed in his lifetime.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: Knowing of a dangerous road over a cliff that had caused the death and injury of many persons he resolved to cut a tunnel through the mountains there.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: Begging food in the daytime, Zenkai worked at night digging his tunnel.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: When thirty years had gone by, the tunnel was 2,280 feet long, 20, feet high, and 30 feet wide.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: Two years before the work was completed, the son of the official he had slain, who was a skillful swordsman found Zenkai out and came to kill him in revenge.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: ‘I will give you my life willingly,’ said Zenkai.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: ‘Only let me finish this work.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 6, sentence number 3 on page 81

page_content: On the day it is completed then you may kill me.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: So the son awaited the day.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: Several months passed and Zenkai kept on digging.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 7, sentence number 3 on page 81

page_content: The son grew tired of doing nothing and began to help with the digging.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 7, sentence number 4 on page 81

page_content: After he had helped for more than a year, he came to admire Zenkai’s strong will and character.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: At last the tunnel was completed and the people could use it and travel in safety.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: ‘Now cut off my head,’ said Zenkai.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 9, sentence number 2 on page 81

page_content: ‘My work is done.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 60. The Tunnel, paragraph number 10, sentence number 1 on page 81

page_content: ‘How can I cut off my own teacher’s head?’ asked the younger man with tears in his eyes.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: The emperor Goyozei was studying Zen under Gudo.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 82

page_content: He inquired: ‘In Zen this very mind is Buddha.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 82

page_content: Is this correct?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: Gudo answered: ‘If I say yes, you will think that you understand without understanding.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 82

page_content: If I say no, I would be contradicting a fact which many understand quite well.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: On another day the emperor asked Gudo: ‘Where does the enlightened man go when he dies?’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: Gudo answered: ‘I know not.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: ‘Why don’t you know?’ asked the emperor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: ‘Because I have not died yet,’ replied Gudo.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: The emperor hesitated to inquire further about these things his mind could not grasp.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 82

page_content: So Gudo beat the floor with his hand as if to awaken him, and the emperor was enlightened!

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 82

page_content: The emperor respected Zen and old Gudo more than ever after his enlightenment, and he even permitted Gudo to wear his hat in the palace in winter.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 61. Gudo and the Emperor, paragraph number 8, sentence number 2 on page 82

page_content: When Gudo was over eighty he used to fall asleep in the midst of his lecture, and the emperor would quietly retire to another room so his beloved teacher might enjoy the rest his ageing body required.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 83

page_content: A great Japanese warrior named Nobunaga decided to attack the enemy although he had only one tenth the number of men the opposition commanded.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 83

page_content: He knew that he would win, but his soldiers were in doubt.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 83

page_content: On the way he stopped at a Shinto shrine and told his man, ‘After I visit the shrine I will toss a coin.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 83

page_content: If head comes we will win; if tails we will loose.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 83

page_content: Destiny holds us in her hand.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 83

page_content: Nobunaga entered the shrine and offered a silent prayer.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 83

page_content: He came forth and tossed a coin.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 83

page_content: Heads appeared.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 3, sentence number 4 on page 83

page_content: His soldiers were so eager to fight that they won their battle easily.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 83

page_content: ‘No one can change the hand of destiny,’ his attendant told him after the battle.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 62. In the Hands of Destiny, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 83

page_content: ‘Indeed not,’ said Nobunaga, showing a coin, which had been doubled, with heads facing either way.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 84

page_content: Gasan instructed his adherents one day: ‘Those who speak against killing and who desire to spare the live of all conscious beings are right.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 84

page_content: It is good to protect even animals and insects.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 84

page_content: But what about those persons who kill time, what about those who are destroying wealth and those who destroy political economy?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 84

page_content: We should not overlook them.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 5 on page 84

page_content: Furthermore, what of the one who preaches without enlightenment?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 63. Killing, paragraph number 1, sentence number 6 on page 84

page_content: He is killing Buddhism.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 85

page_content: Kasan was asked to officiate at the funeral of a provincial lord.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 85

page_content: He had never met lords and nobler before so he was nervous.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 85

page_content: When the ceremony started, Kasan sweated.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 85

page_content: Afterwards, when he had returned, he gathered his pupils together.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 85

page_content: Kasan confessed that he was not yet qualified to be a teacher for he lacked the sameness of bearing in the world of fame that he possessed in the secluded temple.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 85

page_content: Then Kasan resigned and became the pupil of another master.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 64. Kasan Sweated, paragraph number 3, sentence number 4 on page 85

page_content: Eight years later he returned to his former pupils, enlightened.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: A young wife fell sick and was about to die.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: ‘I love you so much,’ she told her husband, ‘I do not want to leave you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 86

page_content: Do not go from me to any other woman.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 86

page_content: If you do, I will return as a ghost and cause you endless trouble.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: Soon the wife passed away.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: The husband respected her last wish for the first three months but then he met another woman and fell in love with her.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 86

page_content: They became engaged to be married.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: Immediately after the engagement a ghost appeared every night to the man blaming him for not keeping his promise.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: The ghost was clever too.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 86

page_content: She told him exactly what had transpired between himself and his new sweetheart.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 3, sentence number 4 on page 86

page_content: Whenever he gave his fiancée a present the ghost would describe it in detail She would even repeat conversations, and it so annoyed the man that he could not sleep.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 3, sentence number 5 on page 86

page_content: Someone advised him to take his problem to a Zen master who lived close to the village.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: At length, in despair, the poor man went to him for help.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: ‘Your former wife became a ghost and knows everything you do,’ commented the master.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 86

page_content: ‘Whatever you do or say, whatever you give your beloved she knows.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 4 on page 86

page_content: She must be a very wise ghost.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 5 on page 86

page_content: Really you should admire such a ghost.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 6 on page 86

page_content: The next time she appears, bargain with her.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 4, sentence number 7 on page 86

page_content: Tell her she knows so much you can hide nothing from her, and that if she will answer you one question, you promise to break your engagement and remain single.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: ‘What is the question I must ask her?’ inquired the man.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: The master replied: Take a large handful of soybeans and ask her exactly how many beans you hold in your hand.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: If she cannot tell you, you will know she is only figment of your imagination and will trouble you no longer.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: The next night, when the ghost appeared the man flattered her and told her that she knew everything.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 86

page_content: ‘Indeed,’ replied the ghost, ‘and I know you went to see that Zen master today.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: ‘And since you know so much,’ demanded the man, ‘tell me how many beans I hold in this hand!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 65. The Subjugation of a Ghost, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 86

page_content: There was no longer any ghost to answer the question.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 88

page_content: Yamaoka Tesshu was a tutor of the emperor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 88

page_content: He was also a master of fencing and a profound student of Zen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 88

page_content: His home was the abode of vagabonds.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 88

page_content: He had but one suit of clothes, for they kept him always poor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 88

page_content: The emperor, observing how worn his garments were, gave Yamaoka some money to buy new ones.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 88

page_content: The next rime Yamaoka appeared he wore the same old outfit.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 88

page_content: ‘What became of the new clothes, Yamaoka?’ asked the emperor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 66.Children of His Majesty, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 88

page_content: ‘I provided clothes for the children of Your Majesty,’ explained Yamaoka.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: In modern times a great deal of nonsense is talked about masters and disciples, and about the inheritance of a master’s teaching by favorite pupils, entitling them to pass the truth on to their adherents.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 89

page_content: Of course Zen should be imparted in this way, from heart to heart, and in the past it was really accomplished.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 89

page_content: Silence and humility reigned rather than profession and assertion.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 89

page_content: The one who received such a teaching kept the matter hidden even after twenty years.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 5 on page 89

page_content: Not until another discovered through his own need that a real master was at hand was it learned that the teaching had been imparted, and even then the occasion arose quite naturally and the teaching made its way in its own right.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 1, sentence number 6 on page 89

page_content: Under no circumstance did the teacher ever claim ‘I am the successor of So-and-so.’ Such a claim would prove quite the contrary

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: The Zen master Mu-nan had only one successor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 89

page_content: His name was Shoju.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 89

page_content: After Shoju had completed his study of Zen.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 4 on page 89

page_content: Mu- nan called him into his room.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 5 on page 89

page_content: ‘I am getting old,’ he said, ‘and as far as I know Shoju, you are the only one who will carry out this teaching.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 6 on page 89

page_content: Here is a book.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 7 on page 89

page_content: It has been passed down from master to master for seven generations.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 8 on page 89

page_content: I also have added many points according to my understanding.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 2, sentence number 9 on page 89

page_content: The book is very valuable and I am giving it to you to represent your successor ship.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: ‘If the book is such an important thing, you had better keep it,’ Shoju replied.’ I received your Zen without writing and am satisfied with it as it is.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: ‘I know that,’ said Mu-nan.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 89

page_content: ‘Even so, this work has been carried from master to master for seven generations, so you may keep it as a symbol of having received the teaching.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 4, sentence number 3 on page 89

page_content: Here.’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: The two happened to be talking before a brazier.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 5, sentence number 2 on page 89

page_content: The instant Shoju felt the book in his hands he thrust it into the flaming coals.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 5, sentence number 3 on page 89

page_content: He had no lust for possessions.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: Mu-nan who never had been angry before yelled: ‘What are you doing!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 67. What Are You Doing! What Are You Saying!, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 89

page_content: Shoju shouted back: ‘What are you saying!’

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 90

page_content: After Kakua visited the emperor he disappeared and no one knew what became of him.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 90

page_content: He was the first Japanese to study Zen in China, but since he showed nothing of it, save one note, he is not remembered for having brought Zen into his country.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 90

page_content: Kakua visited China and accepted the true teaching.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 90

page_content: He did not travel while he was there.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 90

page_content: Meditating constantly, he lived on a remote part of a mountain.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 2, sentence number 4 on page 90

page_content: Whenever people found him and asked him to preach he would say a few words and then move to another part of the mountain where he could be found less easily.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 90

page_content: The emperor heard about Kakua when he returned to Japan and asked him to preach Zen for his edification and that of his subjects.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 90

page_content: Kakua stood before the emperor in silence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 90

page_content: He then produced a flute from the folds of his robe and blew one short note.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 68. One Note of Zen, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 90

page_content: Bowing politely, he disappeared.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 69. Eating the Blame, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 91

page_content: Circumstances arose one day which delayed preparation of the dinner of a Sate Zen master, Fugai, and his followers.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 69. Eating the Blame, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 91
