metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 191

page_content: This heaven is so vast no message can stain it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 191

page_content: How may a snowflake exist in a raging fire?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 1, sentence number 4 on page 191

page_content: Here are the footprints of the patriarchs.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 191

page_content: Comment: Mediocrity is gone.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 191

page_content: Mind is clear of limitation.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 191

page_content: I seek no state of enlightenment.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 4 on page 191

page_content: Neither do I remain where no enlightenment exists.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 5 on page 191

page_content: Since I linger in neither condition, eyes cannot see me.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 8. Both Bull and Self Transcended, paragraph number 2, sentence number 6 on page 191

page_content: If hundreds of birds strew my path with flowers, such praise would be meaningless.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 192

page_content: Too many steps have been taken returning to the root and the source.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 192

page_content: Better to have been blind and deaf from the beginning!

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 192

page_content: Dwelling in one’s true abode, unconcerned with that without — The river flows tranquilly on and the flowers are red.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 192

page_content: Comment: From the beginning, truth is clear.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 192

page_content: Poised in silence, I observe the forms of integration and disintegration.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 9. Reaching the Source, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 192

page_content: One who is not attached to “form” need not be “reformed.” The water is emerald, the mountain is indigo, and I see that which is creating and that which is destroying.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 193

page_content: Barefooted and naked of breast, I mingle with the people of the world.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 1, sentence number 2 on page 193

page_content: My clothes are ragged and dust-laden, and I am ever blissful.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 1, sentence number 3 on page 193

page_content: I use no magic to extend my life; Now, before me, the dead trees become alive.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 193

page_content: Comment: Inside my gate, a thousand sages do not know me.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 193

page_content: The beauty of my garden is invisible.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 193

page_content: Why should one search for the footprints of the patriarchs?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 2, sentence number 4 on page 193

page_content: I go to the market place with my wine bottle and return home with my staff.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: 10. In the World, paragraph number 2, sentence number 5 on page 193

page_content: I visit the wineshop and the market, and everyone I look upon becomes enlightened.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 195

page_content: Transcribed by Paul Reps

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Zen is nothing new, neither is it anything old.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: Long before Buddha was born the search was on in India, as the present work shows.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Long after man has forgotten such words as Zen and Buddha, satori and koan, China and Japan and America—still the search will go on, still Zen will be seen even in flower, and grass-blade, before the sun.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: The following is adapted from the preface to the first version in English of this ancient work.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Wandering in the ineffable beauty of Kashmir, above Srinagar I come upon the hermitage of Lakshmanjoo.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: It overlooks green rice fields, the garden, of Shalimar and Nishat Bagh, lakes fringed with lotus.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: Water streams down from a mountaintop.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Here Lakshmanjoo—tall, full bodied, shining—welcomes me.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: He shares with me this ancient teaching from the Vigyan Bhairava and Sochanda Tantra, both written about four thousand years ago, and from Malini Vijaya Tantra, probably another thousand years older yet.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 7, sentence number 3 on page 196

page_content: It is an ancient teaching, copied and recopied countless times, and from it Lakshmanjoo has made the beginning of an English version.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 7, sentence number 4 on page 196

page_content: I transcribe it eleven more times to get it into the form given here.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Shiva first chanted it to his consort Devi in a language of love we have yet to learn.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 8, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: It is about the Immanent experience.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 8, sentence number 3 on page 196

page_content: It presents 112 ways to open the invisible door of consciousness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 8, sentence number 4 on page 196

page_content: I see Lakshmanjoo gives his life to its practicing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 9, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Some of the ways may appear redundant, yet each differs from any other.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 9, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: Some may seem simple, yet any one requires constant dedication even to test it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 10, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Machines, ledgers, dancers, athletes balance.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 10, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: Just as centering or balance augments various skills, so it may awareness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 10, sentence number 3 on page 196

page_content: As an experiment, try standing equally on both feet; then imagine you are shifting your balance slightly from foot to foot: just as balance centers, do you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 11, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: If we are conscious in part, this implies more inclusive consciousness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 11, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: Have you a hand?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 11, sentence number 3 on page 196

page_content: Yes.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 11, sentence number 4 on page 196

page_content: That you know without doubt.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 11, sentence number 5 on page 196

page_content: But until asked the question were you cognizant of the hand apart?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 12, sentence number 1 on page 196

page_content: Surely men as inspiritors, known and unknown to the world, have shared a common uncommon discovery.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 12, sentence number 2 on page 196

page_content: The Tao of Lao-tse, Nirvana of Buddha, Jehovah of Moses, the Father of Jesus, the Allah of Mohammed — all point to the experience. No-thing-ness, spirit—once touched, the whole life clears.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 13, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: DEVI SAYS:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 14, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: O Shiva, what is your reality?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 15, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: What is this wonder-filled universe?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 16, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: What constitutes seed?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 17, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: Who centers the universal wheel?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 18, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: What is this life beyond form pervading forms?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 19, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: How may we enter it fully, above space and time, names and descriptions?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 20, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: Let my doubts be cleared!

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 21, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: SHIVA REPLIES:

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 22, sentence number 1 on page 198

page_content: [Devi, though already enlightened, has asked the foregoing questions so others through the universe might receive Shiva’s instructions.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 22, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Now follow Shiva’s reply, giving the 112 ways.]

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 23, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 23, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Radiant one, this experience may dawn between two breaths.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 23, sentence number 3 on page 198

page_content: After breath comes in (down) and just before turning up (out) — the beneficence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 24, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 24, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: As breath turns from down to up, and again as breath curves from up to down—through both these turns, realize.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 25, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 25, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Or, whenever inbreath and outbreath fuse, at this instant touch the energyless energy-filled center.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 26, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 26, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Or, when breath is all out (up) and stopped of itself, or all in (down) and stopped—in such universal pause, one’s small self vanishes.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 26, sentence number 3 on page 198

page_content: This is difficult only for the impure.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 27, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 27, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Consider your essence as light rays rising from center to center up the vertebrae, and so rises livingness in you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 28, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 28, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Or in the spaces between, feel this as lightning.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 29, sentence number 1 on page 198
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 29, sentence number 2 on page 198

page_content: Devi, imagine the Sanskrit letters in these honey-filled foci of awareness, first as letters, then more subtly as sounds, then as most subtle feeling.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 29, sentence number 3 on page 198

page_content: Then, leaving them aside, be free.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 30, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 30, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Attention between eyebrows, let mind be before thought.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 30, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: Let form fill with breath-essence to the top of the head, and there shower as light.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 31, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 31, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Or, imagine the five-colored circles of the peacock tail to be your five senses in illimitable space.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 31, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: Now let their beauty melt within.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 31, sentence number 4 on page 199

page_content: Similarly, at any point in space or on a wall — until the point dissolves.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 31, sentence number 5 on page 199

page_content: Then your wish for another comes true.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 32, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 32, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Eyes closed, see your inner being in detail.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 32, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: Thus see your true nature.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 33, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 33, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Place your whole attention in the nerve, delicate as the lotus thread, in the center of your spinal column.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 33, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: In such be transformed.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 34, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 34, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Closing the seven openings of the head with your hands, a space between your eyes becomes all-inclusive.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 35, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 35, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Touching eyeballs as a feather, lightness between them opens into heart and there permeates the cosmos.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 36, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 36, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Bathe in the center of sound, as in the continuous sound of a waterfall.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 36, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: Or, by putting fingers in ears, hear the sound of sounds.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 37, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 37, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Intone a sound, as a-u-m, slowly.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 37, sentence number 3 on page 199

page_content: As sound enters soundfulness, so do you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 38, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 38, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: In the beginning and gradual refinement of the sound of any letter, awake.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 39, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 39, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: While listening to stringed instruments, hear their composite central sound; thus omnipresence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 40, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 40, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Intone a sound audibly, then less and less audibly as feeling deepens into this silent harmony.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 41, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 41, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Imagine spirit simultaneously within and around you until the entire universe spiritualizes.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 42, sentence number 1 on page 199
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 42, sentence number 2 on page 199

page_content: Kind Devi, enter etheric presence pervading far above and below your form.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 43, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 43, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Put mindstuff in such inexpressible fineness above, below, and in your heart.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 44, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 44, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Consider any area of your present form as limitlessly spacious.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 45, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 45, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Feel your substance, bones, flesh, blood, saturated with cosmic essence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 46, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 46, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Suppose your passive form to be an empty room with walls of skin— empty.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 47, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 47, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Blessed one, as senses are absorbed in heart, reach the center of the lotus.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 48, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 48, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Unminding mind, keep in the middle—until.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 49, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 49, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: When in worldly activity, keep attentive between the two breaths, and so practicing, in a few days be born anew.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 49, sentence number 3 on page 200

page_content: [Lakshmanjoo says this is his favorite.]

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 50, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 50, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Focus on fire rising through your form from the toes up until the body burns to ashes but not you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 51, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 51, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Meditate on the make-believe world as burning to ashes, and become being above human.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 52, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 52, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Feel the fine qualities of creativity permeating your breasts and assuming delicate configurations.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 53, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 53, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: With intangible breath in center of forehead, as this reaches heart at the moment of sleep, have direction over dreams and over death itself.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 54, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 54, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: As, subjectively, letters flow into words and words into sentences, and as, objectively, circles flow into worlds and worlds into principles, find at last these converging in our being.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 55, sentence number 1 on page 200
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 55, sentence number 2 on page 200

page_content: Gracious one, play the universe is an empty shell wherein your mind frolics infinitely.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 56, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 56, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Look upon a bowl without seeing the sides or the material.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 56, sentence number 3 on page 201

page_content: In a few moments become aware.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 57, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 57, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Abide in some place endlessly spacious, clear of trees, hills, habitations.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 57, sentence number 3 on page 201

page_content: Thence comes the end of mind pressures.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 58, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 58, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Sweet-hearted one, meditate on knowing and not knowing, existing and not existing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 58, sentence number 3 on page 201

page_content: Then leave both aside that you may be.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 59, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 59, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Look lovingly on some object Do not go on to another object.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 59, sentence number 3 on page 201

page_content: Here, in the middle of this object — the blessing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 60, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 60, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Feel cosmos as translucent ever-living presence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 61, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 61, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: With utmost devotion, center on the two junctions of breath and know the knower.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 62, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 62, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Consider the plenum to be your own body of bliss.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 63, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 63, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: While being caressed, sweet princess, enter the caressing as everlasting life.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 64, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 64, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Stop the doors of senses when feeling the creeping of an ant.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 64, sentence number 3 on page 201

page_content: Then.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 65, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 65, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: At the start of sexual union, keep attention on the fire in the beginning, and, so continuing, avoid the embers in the end.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 66, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 66, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: When in such embrace your senses are shaken as leaves, enter this shaking.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 67, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 67, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: Even remembering union, without the embrace, the transformation.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 68, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 68, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: On joyously seeing a long-absent friend, permeate this joy.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 69, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 69, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: When eating or drinking, become the taste of the food or drink, and be filled.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 70, sentence number 1 on page 201
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 70, sentence number 2 on page 201

page_content: 0 lotus-eyed one, sweet of touch, when singing, seeing, tasting, be aware you are and discover the ever-living.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 71, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 71, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Wherever satisfaction is found, in whatever act, actualize this.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 72, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 72, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: At the point of sleep when sleep has not yet come and external wakefulness vanishes, at this point being is revealed.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 72, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: [Lakshmanjoo says this is another of his favorites.]

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 73, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 73, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: In summer when you see the entire sky endlessly clear, enter such clarity.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 74, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 74, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Lie down as dead.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 74, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: Enraged in wrath, stay so.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 74, sentence number 4 on page 202

page_content: Or stare without moving an eyelash.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 74, sentence number 5 on page 202

page_content: Or suck something and become the sucking.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 75, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 75, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Without support for feet or hands, sit only on buttocks.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 75, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: Suddenly, the centering.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 76, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 76, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: In an easy position, gradually pervade an area between the armpits into great peace.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 77, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 77, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: See as if for the first time a beauteous person or an ordinary object.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 78, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 78, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: With mouth slightly open, keep mind in the middle of tongue.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 78, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: Or, as breath comes silently in, feel the sound HH.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 79, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 79, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: When on a bed or a seat, let yorself become weightless, beyond mind.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 80, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 80, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: In a moving vehicle, by rhythmically swaying, experience.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 80, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: Or in a still vehicle, by letting yourself swing in slowing invisible circles.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 81, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 81, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Simply by looking into the blue sky beyond clouds, the serenity.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 82, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 82, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Shakti, see all space as if already absorbed in your own head in the brilliance.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 83, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 83, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: Waking, sleeping, dreaming, know you as light.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 84, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 84, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: In rain during a black night, enter that blackness as the form of forms.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 85, sentence number 1 on page 202
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 85, sentence number 2 on page 202

page_content: When a moonless raining night is not present, close eyes and find blackness before you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 85, sentence number 3 on page 202

page_content: Opening eyes, see blackness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 85, sentence number 4 on page 202

page_content: So faults disappear forever.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 86, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 86, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Just as you have the impulse to do something, stop.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 87, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 87, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Center on the sound a-u-m without any a or m.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 88, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 88, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Silently intone a word ending in AH.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 88, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: Then in the HH effortlessly, the spontaneity.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 89, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 89, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Feel yourself as pervading all directions, far, near.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 90, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 90, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Pierce some part of your nectar-filled form with a pin, and gently enter the piercing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 91, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 91, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Feel: My thought, I-ness, internal organs—me.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 92, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 92, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Illusions deceive.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 92, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: Colors circumscribe.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 92, sentence number 4 on page 203

page_content: Even divisibles are indivisible.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 93, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 93, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: When some desire comes, consider it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 93, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: Then, suddenly, quit it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 94, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 94, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: Before desire and before knowing, how can I say I am?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 94, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: Consider.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 94, sentence number 4 on page 203

page_content: Dissolve in the beauty.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 95, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 95, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: With your entire consciousness in the very start of desire, of knowing, know.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 96, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 96, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: 0 Shakti, each particular perception is limited, disappearing in omnipotence.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 97, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 97, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: In truth forms are inseparate.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 97, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: Inseparate are omnipresent being and your own form.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 97, sentence number 4 on page 203

page_content: Realize each as made of this consciousness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 98, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 98, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: In moods of extreme desire, be undisturbed.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 99, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 99, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: This so-called universe appears as a juggling, a picture show.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 99, sentence number 3 on page 203

page_content: To be happy look upon it so.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 100, sentence number 1 on page 203
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 100, sentence number 2 on page 203

page_content: 0 Beloved, put attention neither on pleasure or pain but between these.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 101, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 101, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Toss attachment for body aside, realizing I am everywhere.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 101, sentence number 3 on page 204

page_content: One who is everywhere is joyous.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 102, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 102, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Objects and desires exist in me as in others.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 102, sentence number 3 on page 204

page_content: So accepting, let them be translated.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 103, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 103, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: The appreciation of objects and subjects is the same for an enlightened as for an unenlightened person.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 103, sentence number 3 on page 204

page_content: The former has one greatness: he remains in the subjective mood, not lost in things.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 104, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 104, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Feel the consciousness of each person as your own consciousness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 104, sentence number 3 on page 204

page_content: So, leaving aside concern for self, become each being.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 105, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 105, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Thinking no thing, will limited-self unlimit.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 106, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 106, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Believe omniscient, omnipotent, pervading.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 107, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 107, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: As waves come with water and flames with fire, so the universal waves with us.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 108, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 108, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Roam about until exhausted and then, dropping to the ground, in this dropping be whole.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 109, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 109, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Suppose you are gradually being deprived of strength or of knowledge.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 109, sentence number 3 on page 204

page_content: At the instant of deprivation, transcend.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 110, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 110, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Listen while the ultimate mystical teaching is imparted: Eyes still, without winking, at once become absolutely free.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 111, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 111, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Stopping ears by pressing and rectum by contracting, enter the sound of sound.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 112, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 112, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: At the edge of a deep well look steadily into its depths until — the wondrousness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 113, sentence number 1 on page 204
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 113, sentence number 2 on page 204

page_content: Wherever your mind is wandering, internally or externally at this very place, this.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 114, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 114, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: When vividly aware through some particular sense, keep in the awareness.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 115, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 115, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: At the start of sneezing, during fright, in anxiety, above a chasm, flying in battle, in extreme curiosity, at the beginning of hunger, at the end of hunger, be uninterruptedly aware.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 116, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 116, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Let attention be at a place where you are seeing some past happening, and even your form, having lost its present characteristics, is transformed.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 117, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 117, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Look upon some object, then slowly withdraw your sight from it, then slowly withdraw your thought from it.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 117, sentence number 3 on page 205

page_content: Then.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 118, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 118, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Devotion frees.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 119, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 119, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Feel an object before you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 119, sentence number 3 on page 205

page_content: Feel the absence of all other objects but this one.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 119, sentence number 4 on page 205

page_content: Then, leaving aside the object-feeling and the absence-feeling, realize.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 120, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 120, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: The purity of other teachings is as impurity to us.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 120, sentence number 3 on page 205

page_content: In reality know nothing as pure or impure.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 121, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 121, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: This consciousness exists as each being, and nothing else exists.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 122, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 122, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Be the unsame same to friend as to stranger, in honor and dishonor.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 123, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 123, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: When a mood against someone or for someone arises, do not place it on the person in question, but remain centered.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 124, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 124, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Suppose you contemplate something beyond perception, beyond grasping, beyond not being, you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 125, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 125, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Enter space, supportless, eternal, still.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 126, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 126, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Wherever your attention alights, at this very point, experience.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 127, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 127, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: Enter the sound of your name and, through this sound, all sounds.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 128, sentence number 1 on page 205
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 128, sentence number 2 on page 205

page_content: I am existing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 128, sentence number 3 on page 205

page_content: This is mine.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 128, sentence number 4 on page 205

page_content: This is this.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 128, sentence number 5 on page 205

page_content: 0 Beloved, even in such know illimitably. 107.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 129, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: This consciousness is the spirit of guidance of each one.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 129, sentence number 3 on page 206

page_content: Be this one.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 129, sentence number 1 on page 206
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 129, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: Here is a sphere of change, change, change.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 129, sentence number 3 on page 206

page_content: Through change consume change.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 130, sentence number 1 on page 206
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 130, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: As a hen mothers her chicks, mother particular knowings, particular doings, in reality.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 131, sentence number 1 on page 206
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 131, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: Since, in truth, bondage and freedom are relative, these words are only for those terrified with the universe.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 131, sentence number 3 on page 206

page_content: This universe is a reflection of minds.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 131, sentence number 4 on page 206

page_content: As you see many suns in water from one sun, so see bondage and liberation.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 132, sentence number 1 on page 206
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 132, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: Each thing is perceived through knowing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 132, sentence number 3 on page 206

page_content: The self shines in space through knowing.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 132, sentence number 4 on page 206

page_content: Perceive one being as knower and known.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 133, sentence number 1 on page 206
Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: CENTERING, paragraph number 133, sentence number 2 on page 206

page_content: Beloved, at this moment let mind, knowing, breath, form, be included.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 1, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: What Is Zen?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 2, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: Try if you wish.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 2, sentence number 2 on page 207

page_content: But Zen comes of itself.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 2, sentence number 3 on page 207

page_content: True Zen shows in everyday living, CONSCIOUSNESS in action.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 2, sentence number 4 on page 207

page_content: More than any limited awareness, it opens every inner door to our infinite nature.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 3, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: Instantly mind frees.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 3, sentence number 2 on page 207

page_content: How it frees!

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 3, sentence number 3 on page 207

page_content: False Zen wracks brains as a fiction concocted by priests and salesmen to peddle their own wares.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 4, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: Look at it this way, inside out and outside in: CONSCIOUSNESS everywhere, inclusive, through you.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 4, sentence number 2 on page 207

page_content: Then you can’t help living humbly, in wonder.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 5, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: “What is Zen?”

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 6, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: One answer: Inayat Khan tells a Hindu story of a fish who went to a queen fish and asked: “I have always heard about the sea, but what is this sea?

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 6, sentence number 2 on page 207

page_content: Where is it?”

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 7, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: The queen fish explained: “You live, move, and have your being in the sea.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 7, sentence number 2 on page 207

page_content: The sea is within you and without you, and you are made of sea, and you will end in sea.

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 7, sentence number 3 on page 207

page_content: The sea surrounds you as your own being.”

metadata.source: Source document: ZEN FLESH, ZEN BONES, Chapter: What Is Zen?, paragraph number 8, sentence number 1 on page 207

page_content: Another answer: * “Mu” is the negative symbol in Chinese, meaning “No thing” or “Nay.”
